verdicts.reason: Defensible tier-2 attested syncretism. Through Tibetan-Mongolian Buddhist transmission (intensifying 16th-17th c.), the indigenous Tengrist death deity Erlik was directly identified with the Buddhist Yama, taking the title Erlik Nomun Khan ('Erlik, Lord of the Dharma') — a Mongolian calque of Yama's epithet Dharmaraja. This is documented religious contact and borrowing, not a mere structural parallel. Correctly NOT framed as a cognate (the figures are from unrelated families). Tier 2 holds.
